description.value:
Appendix 5 (PF1e prices ÷10): 90 gp per pot; black and white 180 gp. One pot = one application: 1 minute to paint (it provokes nothing but attention); only one color can be active at a time (a new color replaces the old); the magic lasts 24 hours.

  • Black (180 gp) — your outline smears: you are concealed against attacks from farther than 5 feet (as blur).
  • Blue (90 gp) — +10-foot status bonus to your Speeds.
  • Green (90 gp) — the war-chant rides you: +1 status bonus to attack rolls and to saves vs. fear.
  • Orange (90 gp) — resistance 2 to physical damage (stacks with rage-granted resistance).
  • Red (90 gp) — fire resistance 10 (the burn riders' paint).
  • Silver (90 gp) — +1 status bonus to AC.
  • White (180 gp) — Skoan-Quah grave-paint: +2 status to saves vs. death and void effects; the first time you would become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained}, reduce the value by 1 and the paint flakes away spent.
  • Yellow (90 gp) — +2 item bonus to Perception.

Ch.4: Flameford's gratitude includes 5 pots (GM's pick of colors) — this doc supplies the mechanics the chapter grant references.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
